title: Tapping for Body Overwhelm
subtitle: A five-minute tapping practice for when the body feels like too much to manage
excerpt: A five-minute tapping practice drawn from the WEIGHT LOSS program. Use it when the body's demands, changes, or limitations feel unmanageable. The script releases the pressure to fix, control, or understand the body all at once.
sourceNotes: Adapted from Day 3 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), in use since the mid-1990s.

practice.whenToUse: Use when the body's demands feel like too much — when you are exhausted by trying to manage, change, or understand it and need to set the pressure down.

content.text: A five-minute tapping practice for when the body feels like too much to manage all at once. Use it when the demands of a body that won't cooperate, won't settle, or won't look the way you want it to have piled up into one long overwhelm.
New to tapping? Read
how EFT tapping works
first.
The practice
Karate chop (repeat x3)
Even though my body feels like too much right now and I cannot keep up with all it needs, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I am exhausted by trying to manage, change, and understand this body, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Even though I wish I could hand this body back for a simpler one, I deeply and completely accept myself.
Tapping round
**Eyebrow:** This body is so much to manage.
**Side of eye:** I am tired of working around it.
**Under eye:** It keeps changing on me.
**Under nose:** I can't keep up with what it needs.
**Chin:** I am exhausted by it all.
**Collarbone:** I just want it to be easier.
**Under arm:** I want to stop fighting it.
**Top of head:** This is a lot to carry.
Reframe to positive
**Eyebrow:** I give myself permission to stop managing it for five minutes.
**Side of eye:** I can work with it from where I am today.
**Under eye:** My body is doing its best with what it has.
**Under nose:** I am allowed to start from here.
**Chin:** I don't have to fix it all today.
**Collarbone:** I can set the pressure down.
**Under arm:** I am open to a gentler relationship with this body.
**Top of head:** My body and I are on the same side.
Tap each round three times, then move to the reframe and tap that three times.
Where this practice comes from

Adapted from Day 3 of WEIGHT LOSS: A 12-Week Tapping Program (Rebecca J Page, 2025). The tapping framework is Gary Craig's Emotional Freedom Technique (EFT), developed in the mid-1990s from Roger Callahan's Thought Field Therapy.
